ImageDescription.value: Photograph album of German and Austrian scientists. Here: Photo of Eduard Heinrich Mayer, Cöln. Page 21. Publisher, Bookseller, – The album has been sent to Charles Darwin in order to mark his birthday on 12 February 1877 by the civil servant Emil Rade, and contained 165 portraits of German and Austrian scientists. Its ornate frontispiece was designed by the painter Arthur Fitger and dedicated: "Dem Reformator der Naturgeschichte Charles Darwin" (translated: To the reformer of natural history Charles Darwin). Most of the people in the album were faculty members of German and Austrian universities. – Eduard Heinrich Mayer, * 08.03.1821 in Aachen, 1829 baptized Protestant, + 05.08.1907 Köln, son of Jacob Anton Mayer (founder of die "Mayersche", Bookshop in Aachen) und Fanny Feust. Eduard Heinrich Mayer was married to Emilie Poensgen, * 09.08.1830, + 09.10.1885. He used to live in Cologne.

Credit.value: www.darwinproject.ac.uk/people/german-and-dutch-photograph-albums/photograph-album-german-and-austrian-scientists Photograph album of German and Austrian scientists. Album-Author was Emil Rade, (27.11.1832–2.2.1931).
